title: Small sausage in large sausage
summary: Small sausage in large sausage (simplified Chinese: 大肠包小肠; traditional Chinese: 大腸包小腸; pinyin: dàchángbāoxiǎocháng; Pe̍h-ōe-jī: tōa-tn̂g pau sió-tn̂g; Zhuyin Fuhao: ㄉㄚˋㄔㄤˊㄅㄠㄒㄧㄠˇㄔㄤˊ; lit. 'large intestine wrapping small intestine') is a snack sausage sandwich invented in Taiwan in the late 20th century. A segment of Taiwanese pork sausage is wrapped in a (slightly bigger and fatter) sticky rice patty, and usually served chargrilled. Deluxe versions are available in night markets in Taiwan, with condiments such as pickled bokchoy, pickled cucumber, garlic, hot peppers, wasabi, and thick soy sauce paste to complement the taste.
